content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — Camera usage string is generic ("This app needs camera access.") and location string is a literal "TODO" placeholder (App/Info.plist:6,9), with no location feature evident anywhere in the code.
Pierre: Apple rejects purpose strings that don't explain the specific benefit to the user, and "TODO" is an obvious placeholder that will get flagged immediately during review. The camera string should reference the actual document-scanning feature seen in ScannerView.swift (e.g. "SnapScan uses your camera to scan documents"), and since no location feature exists in the code, either implement it or remove NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription entirely.
